title: The Creed
question: What does the Creed begin with?
answer: The Creed begins with the words 'I believe' or 'We believe.'

title: The Creed
question: Why does faith in God demand that we freely acknowledge him and give witness to him?
answer: Because God is the origin and final end of all things, every person is required to freely acknowledge him and to give public witness to him.

title: The Creed
question: What is the first thing to be believed?
answer: The first thing to be believed is that there is one God, who cares for us and rewards us.

title: The Creed
question: Why can God be known with certainty from created things?
answer: Because, starting from created things, God can be known with certainty through the natural use of human reason.

title: The Creed
question: How do we speak about God?
answer: We speak about God in human language, which was assumed by the divine Word in his Incarnation.

title: The Creed
question: What does God reveal to us about himself?
answer: God reveals that he is a Trinity of Persons: the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

title: The Creed
question: Is the Trinity a doctrine of reason or of faith?
answer: The Trinity is a doctrine of divine Revelation. It surpasses the capacities of human reason.

title: The Creed
question: What does the name 'God' mean?
answer: The name 'God' means he who is the fullness of Being, from whom all things receive their existence.

title: The Creed
question: Is Jesus truly God?
answer: Yes, Jesus is truly God. He is the Only Begotten Son of the Father, one in being with the Father.

title: The Creed
question: Who is Jesus Christ?
answer: Jesus Christ is the Eternal Son of God, who became man and is our Lord and Savior.

title: The Creed
question: What does 'Only Begotten Son of God' mean?
answer: It means that Jesus is the natural Son of God, begotten by the Father from all eternity, not made.

title: The Creed
question: What do we mean when we say that Jesus is 'consubstantial' with the Father?
answer: We mean that the Son is of the same substance as the Father, fully divine like the Father.

title: The Creed
question: Who is the Holy Spirit?
answer: The Holy Spirit is the Third Person of the Blessed Trinity, equal to the Father and the Son, who proceeds from both.

title: The Creed
question: What does the Holy Spirit do in the world?
answer: The Holy Spirit, who is the giver of life, sanctifies the world and guides it toward the salvation of souls.

title: The Creed
question: What does the church mean by 'the economy of salvation'?
answer: It means the loving plan of God for the creation and redemption of the world.

title: The Creed
question: What are the divine 'attributes'?
answer: The divine attributes are qualities we attribute to God in human language: life, love, truth, goodness, omnipotence, eternity.

title: The Creed
question: What does it mean that God is 'all-powerful'?
answer: It means that God can do all things that are possible for him, that is, everything that is not contrary to his nature.

title: The Creed
question: Why does God permit evil?
answer: God permits evil in order to respect the freedom he gave to his creatures, and to draw greater goods from the evil.

title: The Angels
question: What are angels?
answer: Angels are pure, non-embodied spiritual beings who honor God and serve him as messengers.

title: The Angels
question: What is the angelic mission?
answer: The mission of angels is to serve Christ and to be ministers of God's plan for salvation.

title: The Angels
question: Are angels present in the life of the church?
answer: Yes, the church honors the angels and invokes their intercession.

title: The Angels
question: What are the fallen angels called?
answer: The fallen angels are called demons or evil spirits.

title: The Angels
question: How did the fall of the angels come about?
answer: They fell through a free and rebellious choice of evil, made by the angel who was created good.

title: The Human Being
question: Why is the human being created in the image of God?
answer: The human being is created in the image of God to know, love, and serve God in this life, and to be happy with him forever in the next.

title: The Human Being
question: What does it mean to have an immortal soul?
answer: It means that the soul, which is the spiritual principle of the human being, does not die with the body.

title: The Human Being
question: What is the human destiny?
answer: The human destiny is to see God and to share in his eternal beatitude.

title: The Human Being
question: What does it mean that man is created male and female?
answer: It means that the human being is ordered to God in a unique way, and that the communion of persons is the foundation of social life.

title: The Fall
question: What was the state of original justice?
answer: Original justice was the state of friendship and sanctifying grace in which Adam and Eve were created.

title: The Fall
question: What was the sin of our first parents?
answer: The sin was disobedience against God by eating the forbidden fruit.

title: The Fall
question: What were the consequences of the sin of our first parents?
answer: Our first parents lost original holiness and justice, and became subject to death, concupiscence, and evil tendencies.

title: The Fall
question: What is original sin?
answer: Original sin is the sin inherited from our first parents, which makes us deprived of original holiness and justice.

title: The Fall
question: What does 'Concupiscence' mean?
answer: Concupiscence means the disordered movements of the sensitive appetite, which inclines us to evil.

title: The Fall
question: Did God abandon man after the fall?
answer: No, God did not abandon man. In his mercy, God promised a Redeemer and called man to conversion.

title: God the Son Incarnate
question: Why did the Son of God become incarnate?
answer: The Son of God became incarnate to save us from sin and to show us the love of God.

title: God the Son Incarnate
question: How is the Virgin Mary connected to the Incarnation?
answer: The Virgin Mary, by the power of the Holy Spirit, conceived Jesus in her womb.

title: God the Son Incarnate
question: What does the name 'Jesus' mean?
answer: 'Jesus' means 'God saves.' It expresses his identity and his mission.

title: God the Son Incarnate
question: Why is Jesus called 'Christ'?
answer: 'Christ' means 'Anointed.' It indicates that Jesus was sent by the Father to be the Messiah.

title: God the Son Incarnate
question: What does 'Only Begotten Son of God' mean?
answer: It means that Jesus is the Son of God in a unique way, born of the Father from all eternity.

title: God the Son Incarnate
question: What does 'Incarnation' mean?
answer: Incarnation means that the Son of God truly became man, without ceasing to be God.

title: The Mysteries of the Life of Christ
question: What are the Mysteries of the Life of Christ?
answer: They are the events of the earthly life of Jesus from his Incarnation to his Ascension.

title: The Mysteries of the Life of Christ
question: What does the Annunciation celebrate?
answer: The Angel Gabriel announced to the Virgin Mary that she would bear the Son of God.

title: The Mysteries of the Life of Christ
question: What does the Nativity celebrate?
answer: The Nativity celebrates the birth of Jesus in Bethlehem, where he was born of the Virgin Mary.

title: The Mysteries of the Life of Christ
question: Why did Jesus undergo the Passion?
answer: Jesus underwent the Passion to suffer and die for our sins, as the Lamb of God.

title: The Mysteries of the Life of Christ
question: What is the Paschal Mystery?
answer: The Paschal Mystery is the mystery of the Passion, Death, Resurrection, and Ascension of Christ.

title: The Mysteries of the Life of Christ
question: What does Christ's Ascension mean?
answer: Christ ascended into heaven in his glorified humanity, to sit at the right hand of the Father.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: What is the Church?
answer: The Church is the People of God, the Temple of the Holy Spirit, and the Mystical Body of Christ.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: Who founded the Church?
answer: Jesus Christ founded the Church by preaching the Gospel and sending the Holy Spirit.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: What are the marks of the Church?
answer: The marks of the Church are that she is One, Holy, Catholic, and Apostolic.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: What does 'Apostolic' mean?
answer: 'Apostolic' means that the Church was founded on the apostles and continues their teaching.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: What is the role of the Pope and the bishops?
answer: The Pope, as successor of Peter, and the bishops, as successors of the apostles, govern the Church.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: What is the communion of saints?
answer: The communion of saints is the sharing of spiritual goods among all members of the Church.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: What is the Assumption of Mary?
answer: The Assumption of Mary is the dogma that the Virgin Mary was taken up body and soul into heaven.

title: The Holy Spirit and the Church
question: What does 'Mary, Mother of the Church' mean?
answer: Mary is the Mother of the Church because she is the Mother of Christ and of his body, the Church.

title: The Last Things
question: What does 'the Last Things' mean?
answer: The Last Things are the events that conclude the history of the world: Death, Judgment, Heaven, Hell.

title: The Last Things
question: What is death?
answer: Death is the separation of the soul from the body, after which each person stands before God for judgment.

title: The Last Things
question: What is the particular judgment?
answer: The particular judgment is the immediate judgment of each soul by God at the moment of death.

title: The Last Things
question: What is heaven?
answer: Heaven is the state of eternal life in which the saints see God face to face and share in his happiness.

title: The Last Things
question: What is hell?
answer: Hell is the state of eternal separation from God, suffered by those who die in mortal sin.

title: The Last Things
question: What is purgatory?
answer: Purgatory is the state of purification for those who die in God's grace but need to be cleansed before entering heaven.

title: The Last Things
question: What is the resurrection of the dead?
answer: The resurrection is the reunion, at the end of time, of body and soul for those who have died.

title: The Last Things
question: What is the Last Judgment?
answer: The Last Judgment is the public judgment of all humanity by Christ at the end of the world.

title: The Paschal Mystery
question: What is the Paschal Mystery?
answer: The Paschal Mystery is the work of salvation accomplished by Jesus Christ through his Passion, Death, Resurrection, and Ascension.

title: The Paschal Mystery
question: How is the Paschal Mystery celebrated in the liturgy?
answer: The Paschal Mystery is celebrated in the Sacraments, especially the Eucharist, and in the liturgical year.

title: The Paschal Mystery
question: What is the liturgical year?
answer: The liturgical year is the unfolding of the mystery of Christ from his Incarnation to his return in glory.

title: The Sacraments
question: What is a Sacrament?
answer: A Sacrament is a sensible sign instituted by Christ to give grace and communicate divine life.

title: The Sacraments
question: How many Sacraments are there?
answer: There are seven Sacraments: Baptism, Confirmation, Eucharist, Penance, Anointing of the Sick, Holy Orders, and Matrimony.

title: The Sacraments
question: What are the Sacraments of Christian Initiation?
answer: They are Baptism, Confirmation, and Eucharist, which lay the foundation of Christian life.

title: The Sacraments
question: What are the Sacraments of Healing?
answer: They are Penance (Reconciliation) and Anointing of the Sick, which heal spiritual and physical illnesses.

title: The Sacraments
question: What are the Sacraments at the Service of Communion?
answer: They are Holy Orders and Matrimony, which serve the mission of the Church and the salvation of others.

title: Baptism
question: What is Baptism?
answer: Baptism is the Sacrament of new life, which washes away original sin and gives sanctifying grace.

title: Baptism
question: Who can receive Baptism?
answer: Any person who has not yet been baptized can receive Baptism, including infants.

title: Baptism
question: Is Baptism necessary for salvation?
answer: Baptism is necessary for salvation for those who can hear the Gospel and have the possibility of asking for it.

title: Confirmation
question: What is Confirmation?
answer: Confirmation is the Sacrament that strengthens the grace of Baptism and imprints a permanent seal.

title: Confirmation
question: What is the effect of Confirmation?
answer: Confirmation increases sanctifying grace, unites us more firmly with Christ, and gives us the Holy Spirit.

title: Confirmation
question: Who administers Confirmation?
answer: The ordinary minister of Confirmation is the bishop; a priest can delegate to administer it in certain cases.

title: Eucharist
question: What is the Eucharist?
answer: The Eucharist is the Sacrament in which Jesus Christ gives his Body and Blood under the appearances of bread and wine.

title: Eucharist
question: What is the Transubstantiation?
answer: Transubstantiation is the change of the entire substance of bread and wine into the Body and Blood of Christ.

title: Eucharist
question: When did Jesus institute the Eucharist?
answer: Jesus instituted the Eucharist on Holy Thursday at the Last Supper, the night before he died.

title: Eucharist
question: What is the Mass?
answer: The Mass is the Sacrifice of the New Covenant in which Christ offers himself to the Father through the ministry of the priest.

title: Eucharist
question: What are the parts of the Mass?
answer: The Mass has two main parts: the Liturgy of the Word and the Liturgy of the Eucharist.

title: Eucharist
question: What is the real presence of Christ in the Eucharist?
answer: Christ is truly, really, and substantially present under the appearances of bread and wine.

title: Penance
question: What is the Sacrament of Penance?
answer: Penance is the Sacrament of forgiveness, in which sins committed after Baptism are absolved.

title: Penance
question: What are the essential elements of Penance?
answer: The essential elements are contrition, confession, and satisfaction (or penance).

title: Penance
question: What is mortal sin?
answer: Mortal sin is a grave offense against God that destroys charity in the heart and causes loss of sanctifying grace.

title: Penance
question: What is venial sin?
answer: Venial sin is a less serious offense that allows charity to remain, though wounded.

title: Anointing of the Sick
question: What is the Anointing of the Sick?
answer: It is the Sacrament that gives grace to those suffering from illness, to unite their suffering to Christ.

title: Anointing of the Sick
question: Who can receive the Anointing of the Sick?
answer: Any Catholic who is in danger of death from illness or old age can receive this Sacrament.

title: Holy Orders
question: What is Holy Orders?
answer: Holy Orders is the Sacrament by which men are ordained as bishops, priests, or deacons.

title: Holy Orders
question: What are the three degrees of Holy Orders?
answer: The three degrees are Deacon, Priest, and Bishop.

title: Holy Orders
question: What is the role of the priest?
answer: Priests are ordained to preach the Gospel, celebrate the Sacraments, especially the Eucharist and Penance.

title: Matrimony
question: What is the Sacrament of Matrimony?
answer: Matrimony is the covenant by which a man and a woman establish a partnership of the whole life, ordered to the good of the spouses and the procreation of children.

title: Matrimony
question: What are the properties of Christian marriage?
answer: Christian marriage has the properties of unity and indissolubility, by which the spouses are joined forever.

title: Matrimony
question: What is the purpose of Matrimony?
answer: The purposes are the good of the spouses, the procreation and education of children, and the benefit of the Church.

title: The Dignity of the Human Person
question: What is the source of human dignity?
answer: Human dignity comes from God, who created man and woman in his own image and likeness.

title: The Dignity of the Human Person
question: What does it mean to be created in the image of God?
answer: To be created in God's image means that man and woman have intellect and free will, and are called to share in God's divine life.

title: The Dignity of the Human Person
question: What is the moral conscience?
answer: Moral conscience is the judgment of reason by which man recognizes the moral quality of an action.

title: The Dignity of the Human Person
question: What does freedom mean?
answer: Freedom is the faculty of choosing what is good, acting like a son of God and attaining eternal happiness.

title: The Dignity of the Human Person
question: What is the relationship between freedom and truth?
answer: Freedom is ordered to truth: when man acts without truth, he loses his freedom and becomes a slave to error and passion.

title: The Dignity of the Human Person
question: What is the relationship between freedom and law?
answer: True freedom is helped by law, which directs it according to its final purpose.

title: The Human Community
question: What is the common good?
answer: The common good is the sum of social conditions that allow people, either as groups or as individuals, to reach their fulfillment more fully and more easily.

title: The Human Community
question: What is the relationship between the common good and the person?
answer: The common good is not an end in itself; it is ordered to the person, in whom it finds its full meaning.

title: The Human Community
question: What is social justice?
answer: Social justice consists in the concrete realization of the equal dignity of all in rights and duties.

title: God's Commandments
question: Why is the moral law necessary?
answer: The moral law is necessary to ensure that the choices of freedom are oriented toward the good and are not darkened by error or passion.

title: God's Commandments
question: What is the natural moral law?
answer: The natural moral law is the participation of the eternal law in the rational creature, which enables reason to discern good from evil.

title: God's Commandments
question: What is the relationship between the Old Law and the New Law?
answer: The Old Law is the first stage of Revelation; the New Law (the law of the Gospel) is the law of the Holy Spirit.

title: The First Commandment
question: What does 'I am the Lord your God' mean?
answer: It means that God has revealed his name and his nature to us, and we belong to him.

title: The First Commandment
question: What is required by faith?
answer: Faith requires accepting and confessing the name of Jesus Christ, and living according to his Gospel.

title: The First Commandment
question: What is the sin of idolatry?
answer: Idolatry is the worship of a creature in place of God, denying the one true God.

title: The First Commandment
question: What is superstition?
answer: Superstition is the deviation of religious sentiment in practices that dishonor God.

title: The First Commandment
question: What is the sin of irreligion?
answer: The sins of irreligion are blasphemy, perjury, sacrilege, and witchcraft.

title: The First Commandment
question: What is hope?
answer: Hope is the theological virtue by which we desire the kingdom of heaven and eternal life as our happiness.

title: The First Commandment
question: What are the theological virtues?
answer: The theological virtues are Faith, Hope, and Charity, which are directly infused by God into our souls.

title: The First Commandment
question: What is charity?
answer: Charity is the theological virtue by which we love God above all things and our neighbor as ourselves.

title: The First Commandment
question: Why is charity the greatest of the theological virtues?
answer: Because charity unites us to God and is the form of all the virtues; without charity, faith and hope are lifeless.

title: The Second Commandment
question: What does 'You shall not take the name of the Lord your God in vain' mean?
answer: It means that we must use God's name with reverence and never abuse it.

title: The Second Commandment
question: What are violations of the Second Commandment?
answer: They include blasphemy, perjury, and any disrespectful use of God's name.

title: The Third Commandment
question: What does 'Remember to keep holy the Sabbath day' mean?
answer: It means that we must sanctify Sunday, the day of the Resurrection, as the Lord's Day.

title: The Third Commandment
question: What is the Sunday obligation?
answer: On Sundays and holy days of obligation, the faithful are obliged to participate at Mass and to refrain from servile work.

title: The Fourth Commandment
question: What does 'Honor your father and your mother' mean?
answer: It means to love and respect our parents and all those whom God has put in authority over us.

title: The Fourth Commandment
question: What do children owe their parents?
answer: Children owe their parents love, respect, obedience, and practical support in times of need.

title: The Fourth Commandment
question: What do parents owe their children?
answer: Parents owe their children spiritual and material formation, including education, discipline, and good example.

title: The Fourth Commandment
question: What is the domestic church?
answer: The family is called the domestic church, where the faith is first learned and lived.

title: The Fifth Commandment
question: What does 'You shall not kill' mean?
answer: It means that we must respect human life and not harm our neighbor in body or soul.

title: The Fifth Commandment
question: What are the grave sins against life?
answer: Grave sins against life include murder, genocide, abortion, euthanasia, and suicide.

title: The Fifth Commandment
question: What is the duty to protect the innocent?
answer: We have a duty to protect the lives of the innocent, especially the unborn and the most vulnerable.

title: The Fifth Commandment
question: What is the right to self-defense?
answer: A person has the right to defend his own life, but not to kill except in cases of legitimate defense.

title: The Fifth Commandment
question: What is the duty to care for one's health?
answer: We have a duty to care for our physical and spiritual health, avoiding excess and harm.

title: The Fifth Commandment
question: What are violence and abuse?
answer: Violence and abuse are grave sins that wound human dignity and are condemned by the Gospel.

title: The Sixth and Ninth Commandments
question: What does 'You shall not commit adultery' mean?
answer: It means that marriage is sacred and exclusive, and that sexual morality must be respected.

title: The Sixth and Ninth Commandments
question: What is the virtue of chastity?
answer: Chastity is the moral virtue that moderates the desire for pleasure and regulates the use of sexuality.

title: The Sixth and Ninth Commandments
question: What are sins against chastity?
answer: They include fornication, pornography, prostitution, adultery, homosexuality, and lust.

title: The Sixth and Ninth Commandments
question: What is the purpose of sexuality?
answer: Sexuality is ordered to the love of husband and wife and to the procreation and education of children.

title: The Sixth and Ninth Commandments
question: What does the Church teach about homosexuality?
answer: Homosexual acts are contrary to the natural law and are not approved; persons with homosexual tendencies must be treated with respect.

title: The Seventh and Tenth Commandments
question: What does 'You shall not steal' mean?
answer: It means that we must respect the goods of our neighbor and not take what does not belong to us.

title: The Seventh and Tenth Commandments
question: What is the social doctrine of the Church?
answer: The social doctrine of the Church is the coherent set of teachings on social, economic, and political questions.

title: The Seventh and Tenth Commandments
question: What is the principle of the universal destination of goods?
answer: God intended the earth and all that is in it for all people, so that all may share in its goods.

title: The Seventh and Tenth Commandments
question: What is the duty to pay just wages?
answer: Workers have a right to wages that are sufficient to support themselves and their families in dignity.

title: The Seventh and Tenth Commandments
question: What is the duty to protect the environment?
answer: We have a duty to protect the environment for ourselves and for future generations.

title: The Eighth Commandment
question: What does 'You shall not bear false witness' mean?
answer: It means that we must always tell the truth and protect the good name of our neighbor.

title: The Eighth Commandment
question: What are violations of the Eighth Commandment?
answer: They include lying, detraction, calumny (slander), and rash judgment.

title: The Eighth Commandment
question: What is the duty to seek truth?
answer: Every person has the duty to seek the truth, especially about God and his Church.

title: Prayer
question: What is prayer?
answer: Prayer is the raising of the heart and mind to God in praise, petition, contrition, and thanksgiving.

title: Prayer
question: What are the forms of prayer?
answer: The forms of prayer are blessing, adoration, petition, intercession, thanksgiving, and praise.

title: Prayer
question: What is vocal prayer?
answer: Vocal prayer is prayer expressed with words, whether spoken aloud or silently.

title: Prayer
question: What is meditative prayer?
answer: Meditative prayer is prayer in which the mind uses reflection on the mysteries of faith to stir the heart.

title: Prayer
question: What is contemplative prayer?
answer: Contemplative prayer is a gaze of faith fixed on God, a loving attention to him.

title: Prayer
question: What is the 'Jesus Prayer'?
answer: The Jesus Prayer is a short prayer often repeated: 'Lord Jesus Christ, Son of God, have mercy on me, a sinner.'

title: The Our Father
question: What is the 'Lord's Prayer'?
answer: The Lord's Prayer, or 'Our Father,' is the perfect prayer that Jesus taught his disciples.

title: The Our Father
question: What does 'Our Father' mean?
answer: 'Our Father' means that God is the Father of all people and especially of the baptized.

title: The Our Father
question: What do we ask for in the first three petitions?
answer: We ask that God's name be holy, that his kingdom come, and that his will be done.

title: The Our Father
question: What do we ask for in the last four petitions?
answer: We ask for our daily bread, the forgiveness of sins, protection in temptation, and delivery from evil.

title: The Our Father
question: What does 'Give us this day our daily bread' mean?
answer: It means that we ask God for everything we need in body and spirit for this day.

title: The Our Father
question: What does 'Forgive us our trespasses' mean?
answer: It means that we ask God to forgive our sins in the measure that we forgive those who sin against us.

title: The Our Father
question: What does 'Lead us not into temptation' mean?
answer: It means that we ask God not to let us be led into serious sin, especially against our faith.

title: The Our Father
question: What does 'Deliver us from evil' mean?
answer: It means that we ask God to free us from all evil, present and future, and especially from the evil of sin.

title: The Doxology
question: What is the doxology of the Our Father?
answer: The doxology is 'For the kingdom, the power, and the glory are yours, now and forever.'

title: The Doxology
question: Why do we say 'Amen' at the end of prayer?
answer: 'Amen' means 'so be it' and expresses our trust and hope that God will hear our prayer.
